title: Matthew Brannon
description: Matthew Brannon has long been recognized not only for his wit and literary sensibility but also for the precision with which he approaches his chosen mediums. He is perhaps best known for his radical approach to printmaking, which, contrary to traditional usage, frequently involves the elaborate production of unique artworks. The vocabulary and voice developed in the prints–arch and erudite, with a sharply psychoanalytic bent–have provided the center for an expanding world of objects and narratives that also includes painting, sculpture, video, and installation. Learn more about his works: matthewbrannon.com Matthew Brannon – MoT+++ A. title: Scott Anderson
description: Scott Anderson is an artist based in Australia. His art practice is concerned with exploring painting, collage and sculpture, working collectively as a hybridised practice. Through his studio work Scott’s research investigates architecture, place/non-place, transition/flux, construction/deconstruction and how cultural identities can influence one’s understanding of place. When creating work, Scott will often use locally reclaimed materials and is not concerned with a preconceived outcome but prefers to develop “a call and response” methodology, allowing each piece to develop organically, taking on a life of its own. Scott Anderson – MoT+++ A. Farm International Art Residency a Saigon-based art residency, in partnership with the Goethe-Institut Ho Chi Minh City, and support from Amanaki Thao Dien Programs Residents News & Events Scott Anderson Scott Anderson is an artist based in Australia. title: MoT sound #9 | aConvergence
description: english | tiếng việt MoT sound event series will re-open for 2019 hosting a night of exploratory sounds with three acts from Vietnam and abroad. ever-encouraging forays into the new, this edition of MoT will feature solo performances from Saigon’s own Jung Buffalo the under underground kid wonder, Linhhafornow back from touring Asia to visit us from Hanoi and Llama / Olo, a very special guest duo from Switzerland. Jung Buffalo is one of the many aliases of Đỗ Tấn Sĩ, b. 1997, a musician / music producer from Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. as Si’s ongoing solo project, Jung Buffalo’s work has explored many forms & genres from ambient noisescapes to glitch trap, minimal acoustic-electric excursions, dronestep, killer-barber-hop, contemporary classical. Si is also the founding member of Ran Cap Duoi Collective . he sometimes wears hats. reaching deep into her soul LinhHafornow transports audiences through wondrous, uplifting and reflective emotional moments. she blends ambient harmonies, traditional instruments and soaring vocals into smooth arrangements of original songs. with a ‘loopstation’, an electronic device more popular with musicians from outside the ambient scene, she uses multiple microphones to capture her voice and a variety of traditional instruments. float through an imagined dreamscape… guided by Linh Hà … for now. check SoundCloud and Vimeo for more on LinhHafornow. soundcloud.com/linhha vimeo.com/user77196635 LLAMA / OLO is an encounter between two activists of the underground, on one side Didier Severin, leader of the terrifying Knut who has for 18 years plowed deep the wild lands of metal across the planet, and on the other the adventurous Loïc Grobéty who has dragged his hump from black metal to tight rock via contemporary music. these 2 protagonists crush the matter, violent it and push the sound in its last retrenchments. the result is dark but cinematographic, powerful and slow. sound penetrates and embraces you to give you a unique sensory experience. line up : Didier Séverin – synthé modulaire Loïc Grobéty – électrique corde piano+électronique curated by Alec Schachner (Scobi Wan / Ech Ech Studios) Tuesday, March 19 7:00 – 10:00 pm location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City. entrance: 100.000 VND MoT sound #9 | aConvergence – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #9 | aConvergence
description: english | tiếng việt chuỗi sự kiện âm thanh MoT sẽ mở lại cho năm 2019 bằng một đêm khám phá âm thanh với ba nhóm nhạc tới từ Việt Nam và nước ngoài. hướng tới những đột phá mới, dịp này MoT âm thanh đưa tới màn trình diễn solo bởi cậu bé kỳ diệu của giới underground Jung Buffalo từ Sài Gòn, Linhhafornow tới từ Hà Nội, trở về từ chuyến lưu diễn châu Á để ghé thăm chúng tôi và Llama / Olo, một bộ đôi rất đặc biệt đến từ Thụy sĩ. Jung Buffalo là một trong nhiều bí danh của Đỗ Tấn Sĩ, sn.1997, một nhạc sĩ / nhà sản xuất âm nhạc từ thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Việt Nam. như là một dự án solo tiếp diễn của Sĩ, các sản phẩm của Jung Buffalo khám phá nhiều hình thức & amp; từ các thể loại như ambient noisescapes đến glitch trap, minimal acoustic-electric excursions, dronestep, killer-barber-hop, contemporary classical. Sĩ cũng là thành viên sáng lập của nhóm Rắn Cạp Đuôi thỉnh thoảng anh đội mũ. đi sâu vào tâm hồn cô ấy LinhHafornow chuyển tiếp tới khán giả những khoảnh khắc cảm xúc kỳ diệu, bay bổng và phản ảnh. cô pha trộn hài hòa giữa môi trường xung quanh, nhạc cụ truyền thống và giọng hát cao vút để tạo thành sự thoả hiệp uyển chuyển với các bài nhạc nguyên bản. cùng với ‘loopstation’, một thiết bị điện tử phổ biến hơn đối với các nhạc sĩ từ bên ngoài dòng ambient, cô dùng nhiều micro để nắm bắt giọng hát và các loại nhạc cụ truyền thống. bồng bềnh trên một khung cảnh mộng tưởng tưởng tượng được dẫn dắt bởi Linh Hà..tạm thời là thế. tham khảo SoundCloud và Vimeo để biết thêm về LinhHafornow. soundcloud.com/linhha vimeo.com/user77196635 LLAMA / OLO là cuộc gặp gỡ giữa hai nhà hoạt động của giới underground, một mặt Didier Severin, lãnh đạo của Knut tồi tệ đã có 18 năm cày sâu trong vùng hoang dã bên mảng kim loại trên khắp hành tinh và mặt khác Loïc Grobéty thích phiêu lưu, người đã thông qua âm nhạc đương đại để kéo cái bướu của mình từ black metal tới tight rock. 2 nhân vật chủ đạo này nghiền nát vật chất, bạo lực chúng và đẩy âm thanh đến thành luỹ cuối cùng của nó. kết quả thì tối nhưng đầy tính điện ảnh, hùng cường và chậm rãi. âm thanh thâm nhập và bao vây lấy bạn đưa tới bạn một trải nghiệm độc đáo. line up: Didier Séverin – synthé modulaire Loïc Grobéty – électrique corde piano+électronique giám tuyển bởi Alec Schachner (Scobi Wan / Ech Ech Studios) thứ ba, 19 tháng 03 7:00 – 10:00 pm địa điểm: Saigon Domaine, Tầng trệt, 1057 Bình Quoi, Phường 28, quận Bình Thạnh, thành phố Hồ Chí Minh. vé vào cửa: 100.000 đ MoT sound #9 | aConvergence – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: Wu Chi-Tsung
description: Wu Chi-Tsung is a Taiwanese visual artist based in Taipei. His practice spans photography, video, and installation, with his experimental Cyano-Collage series exploring the intersection of traditional Chinese painting aesthetics and contemporary photographic processes.

title: Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl
description: Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl both live and work in France. Besides pursuing their own art practice, a.k.a. photography, video, installation and painting, sculpture, and installation, both artists work since 2016 together on collaborative projects exploring the idea of social sculpture. Based on modular structures and the idea of the architecture of additions, they create physical platforms for dialogue and discussion, inspiring towards the idea of communal interaction and reflection, exploration and re-/construction, cultural exchange and togetherness. Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl – MoT+++ A. title: artist talk | collaborative performance | Singapore
description: introduction | participants artists talk is a collaborative ongoing performance piece by artists Aliansyah Caniago and Cam Xanh, conceived during Alinsayah’s residency at MoT+++. it was first performed in March 2018 in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam as part of performance plus, a six-month performance art programme at MoT+++ running from March to August 2018. spreading globally, like the phenomenon from which its name derives, artists talk was conceived to be an ongoing series of utterances, with one performance per city worldwide. by gathering artists in a room to talk but removing their audience, the performance questions the very concept of the “artist talk”, and the obtuse nature of “talk” itself. in requiring artists to continually vocalise their internal dialogues, it turns the thought inside out, and asks whether they really have something to say or if it’s just talking for the sake of talking. a three-channel video, with footage of previous talk, was installed in the room creating a clamour of constant noise. the multiple harmonies and dissonances of audible layers formed from this orchestra of incessant talk giving artists a platform to speak freely from cultural or political censorship, whilst simultaneously eradicating the possibility for them to be heard. the second iteration of artists talk took place in Singapore as part of the S.E.A. Focus art fair. artists working in any discipline were invited to join Aliansyah Caniago, Cam Xanh and the MoT+++ collective, on Friday, January 25 2019 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. replicating the first performance, participants were encouraged to talk simultaneously for two hours, and follow whatever vocal urges emerged. before entering, they were given a printed copy of the freedom of expression manifesto written by Cam Xanh, which set out a number of performance directives dictating their behaviour once inside the room. the manifesto inhibited artists from making eye contact or touching each other, as well as direct responses, although mimicry and provocation of other artists was allowed. most importantly, participants were required to stay inside the locked room for the entire two hours of the performance. nine participants took part in the performance: four MoT+++ collective, four guest artists, including an art technician-cum-artist, and one curator. only participants were allowed in the room, with audience members restricted to watching through eye-level windows, unable to distinguish the murmurings inside. the purpose-built room was located adjacent to the S.E.A. Spotlight Talks, where industry and artists panels and talks were staged throughout the fair from January 24-27. in an echo of the initial performance, some artists began to draw with charcoal on the walls. this action was extended in the performance’s second occurance to drawing on the windows with lipstick and markers also. artists used the paper articulations of the freedom of expression manifesto to cover the windows, further nullifying the

title: Scott Farrand
description: Scott is an emerging artist from Adelaide in South Australia. Born in 1954 he has a Bachelor of Visual Art and Design from the Adelaide College of the Arts majoring in Sculpture. He has had several joint exhibitions in his home state; most notably as a winner in the Waterhouse Natural Science Art Prize. His painting and sculptural art works are expressions of an ongoing interest in the values that underpin and determine the nature of the societies in which we live. His work explores the relationship we have with materialism and its many manifestations; consumerism, the search for happiness, social inclusion, poverty, political spin, climate change and the environment. Scott works with a wide range of materials such as LED lights, recycled plastics and discarded objects but always keeps the joy of making as the central component. Scott Farrand – MoT+++ A. title: Latthapon Korkiatarkul
description: Latthapon Korkiatarkul has exhibited extensively in Southeast Asia since he graduated with his BFA in Fine Arts in 2010 and had his first solo show, (Un)Composition, in 2017. Latthapon’s work often revolves around the desacralization of everyday and art objects, highlighting the importance of mental processes and humour in his practice. Latthapon Korkiatarkul – MoT+++ A. title: Nguyen Duc Phuong
description: Nguyen Duc Phuong uses natural colour pigments originating from soil, flowers and plants for his paintings on ‘dzó’ paper (traditional handmade paper from northern Vietnam), creating works that are delicate and full of life. title: Cian Duggan
description: Cian Duggan was brought up on skate culture during his teenage years. Drawing inspiration from its bootleg and DIY culture, he mixes it up with an interest in industrial materials and design, combining organic yet alien shapes with human and figurative elements. Most of his work is on the walls and streets of places he likes to visit. Learn more about his works: cian-duggan.com Cian Duggan – MoT+++ A. title: entrances | Cian Duggan
description: “entrances’” by Cian Duggan opened as MoT+++ third +1 museum by any other name on Friday November 23, 2018. as pair of works on a busy highway in Saigon one ‘entrance’ had already collapsed by the time of the opening. the second ‘entrance’ closed unfortunately the next day. part 2 “exit” will appear at an unforseen time in the future. sent floating into the sea at Hue, anyone that ‘entered’ via any one of Duggan’s works could eventually ‘exit’ in an unknown place and at an unknown time. Duggan’s abstract forms appear otherworldly, they hover with a static tension that animates them, as extra-terrestrial life forms, or portals to another dimension. yet they coexist with their surroundings, rather than overtake it. somewhere between memory and imagination, primitive and futuristic, organic and digital.. the evocation of the familiar rendered unfamiliar lending the forms their oddness. part 1 of Duggan’s +1 museum by any other name materialises as multiple “entrances”; gateways suggesting disruptive possibilities to the linearity of time and space. their incongruity makes them strangely recognisable as they allude to the many other similar “entrances” hidden in plain sight amongst Saigon’s streets. although there is the possibility that old entrances may close, consumed by the rapidly developing urban environment, Duggan’s compulsion to paint means that new entrances are continually, if only briefly, emerging, waiting to be found. the “entrances” diptych is part 1 of Duggan’s work for MoT+++’s +1 museum by any other name. part 2 “exit” is a singular piece planned for an unforeseen time in the future—a yet to be realised second half of the work that will join other floating artworks in the sea off the coast of Hue, in a venture organised by fellow +1 museum artist Dao Tung. one year after its construction, part 2 will be untethered and released into the ocean creating an unknowable “exit”, drifting in both time and space. entrances | Cian Duggan – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: Tricia Nguyen Thanh Trang
description: Tricia Nguyen Thanh Trang was born in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Major in Textile Design and Fashion at Huddersfield University (England), she has worked as Visual Merchandiser and Buyer before coming back to Vietnam. Tricia has graduated with excellence from the HCMC Dance Academy and has performed for the Ho Chi Minh City Symphony Ballet and Opera. Currently Artistic Director for The Open House Gallery, she has independently founded Wintercearig Project to raise awareness for mental health in Vietnam through contemporary art Tricia Nguyen Thanh Trang – MoT+++ A. title: MoT sound #8 | Amoeba
description: for the 8th session of the MoT sound experiment, MoT+++ will present a 48-hour durational performance titled Amoeba by Saigon-based sound collective Rắn Cạp Đuôi. in the spirit of collaboration and inclusivity, all artists in Saigon are invited to come and participate in the performance; whether a professional musician, sound artist or not. willing participants are welcome to bring an instrument or just themselves, and to jam with Rắn Cạp Đuôi at any point in the performance, helping to keep the concert going for the full 48 hours as band members take it in turns to sleep. other forms of creative expression are also welcome: poets are invited to read, dancers are invited to dance, and there will be materials provided for visual artists to paint or draw during the performance. from Saturday, August 11 (2:00 pm) to Monday, August 13 (2:00 pm) location: A. title: It seems to be | Dao Tung
description: english | tiếng việt the installation of ‘It seems to be’, a video work by Dao Tung at Hoàng Thị- phân đà Mùa Hè Tan Nát Coffee Shop marks MoT+++’s first +1 museum by any other name. up the banyan, the ant climbs alas, dead-end, futile crawl up the peach tree, the ant creeps broken branches, to-and-fro (artist’s statement) about the artist Dao Tung (b. 1983, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam). his work experiments with sound, installation and video mediums that draw from his practice as a music composer and sound artist. a graduate of the University of Economics in Ho Chi Minh City, Dao Tung has gone on to collaborate on performances at home in Vietnam, as well as in Korea and the USA. MoT+++’s +1 museum by any other name is a project of multiples. it exhibits single works in separate locations across the globe, for a minimum of one year. a durational experience, +1 museum by any other name examines the relationship between work, audience and environment and uses the extended period of time to challenge the experience of each component. thank you to our sponsors the Nguyen Art Foundation for loaning the artwork, and to Hoàng Thị- phân đà Mùa Hè Tan Nát for donating the space. location: Hoang Thi Coffee Shop, 14 Ton That Dam, D1, HCMC check Hoàng Thị- phân đà Mùa Hè Tan Nát for opening hours It seems to be | Dao Tung – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. Con kiến mà leo cành đa Leo phải cành cụt leo ra leo vào Con kiến mà leo cành đào Leo phải cành cụt leo vào leo ra (tuyên bố của nghệ sĩ) nghệ sĩ Đào Tùng (sinh năm 1983, thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Việt Nam). tác phẩm của Đào Tùng thử nghiệm với âm thanh, sắp đặt và video rút ra từ những thực hành của anh với tư cách nhà soạn nhạc và nghệ sĩ âm thanh. tốt nghiệp Đại học Kinh tế Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, Đào Tùng đã hợp tác biểu diễn tại Việt Nam, cũng như ở Hàn Quốc và Mỹ Một bảo tàng với bất kể cái tên nào khác của Mọt+++ là một dự án của bội số. chúng tôi trưng bày các tác phẩm đơn lẻ ở các nơi trên toàn cầu, với thời gian tối thiểu là một năm. là một trải nghiệm thời gian, Một bảo tàng với bất kể cái tên nào khác tạo lập mối quan hệ giữa tác phẩm, người xem và cảnh quan xung quanh và sử dụng khoảng thời gian dài để thách thức trải nghiệm của mỗi yếu tố. xin cảm ơn các nhà tài trợ của MọT+++, The Nguyen Art Foundation đã cho mượn tác phẩm và Hoàng Thị- phân đà Mùa Hè Tan Nát tặng cho không gian sắp đặt. địa điểm: Cà phê Hoàng Thị, 14 Tôn Thất Đạm, quận 1, tp HCM mời bạn xem Hoàng Thị- phân đà Mùa Hè Tan Nát để biết giờ mở cửa It seems to be | Đào Tùng – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #7
description: The seventh edition of the MoT sound experiment took place at the soft opening of A. Farm, a new residency space jointly run by MoT+++, San Art, and the Nguyen Art Foundation. The performances took place in the brand new A. Farm exhibition/event space, serving as a closing event at the end of the night, and featured the following artists: Thierry Gotteland-Bernard, a French-born sound artist based in Saigon; Eddie Ladoir, a French sound artist, and a resident artist of the Villa Saigon art residency program sponsored by L’institut Francais; SoundMasons, a collaboration between Saigon based sound artist Alex McCarl and Hanoi based sound artist Alec Schachner ; and special guest Nguyễn Đỗ Minh Quân, one of the leading sound artists based in Hanoi. title: A. Farm | Soft Opening
description: The soft opening of A. Farm, MoT+++'s international art residency program.

title: All Animals Are Equal #1
description: English | Tiếng Việt To celebrate the soft launch of A. Farm—a new artist residency program in Ho Chi Minh City, jointly run by the Nguyen Art Foundation, MoT+++, and Sàn Art—this is an open call invitation to ANY and ALL visual artists working in Ho Chi Minh city to participate in the group exhibition “all animals are equal.” In the spirit of friendship and equality, artists working in any medium, at any point in their career—whether professional or non-professional, young or old, emerging or established—are invited to bring 1-3 artworks, and to install them anywhere they wish at the A. Farm residency complex. Space will be allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Some possible locations where artists may install their work include the studio spaces, the residential spaces, and the exhibition hall/event space (see photos in pinned post). Video artists may submit videos to be included in a group screening. The opening party will also feature a Kirigami workshop, artists’ performances in the studio building, drinks served by Cu Ru bar, and a series of sound art performances to close out the evening. This is an opportunity for the diverse art community of Saigon to come together and share their work in an atmosphere of egalitarian acceptance. Participating artists will be announced as they confirm. This event is partly sponsored by Institut français & Villa Saigon. HOW TO PARTICIPATE: If you wish to exhibit work in the show, simply show up and install your work at A. Farm (96/1 Tân Thới Nhất 6, Khu Phố 6, phường Tân Thới Nhất, Quận 12, TPHCM) between the hours of 10:00 am and 8:00 pm on the 17th, 18th, 19th, and 20th of June. We will have a some basic tools available, such as a hammer, a drill, and a ladder, but artists will be responsible for all their own installation materials (eg. extension cables, screws, cleats, etc.; whatever materials you need you must bring yourself). VIDEO ART: Artists who wish to have video work included in a group-screening, please submit video files to a.farm.saigon@gmail.com by the 18th of June. WORK INFORMATION: Participants are requested to email their artist name, work titles, and prices (if you wish to sell) to a.farm.saigon@gmail.com. Fifty percent of the proceeds from any work sold from this show will go to benefit A. Farm, so please price your work accordingly. OPENING & HOURS: The opening party will be held on June 23rd from 4-10pm, and the exhibit will be open to the public from 12:00 pm to 5:00 pm from June 27th through June 30th. DE-INSTALLATION: Artists are requested to deinstall their work on July 2nd, 3rd, or 4th, between the hours of 02:00 pm and 8:00 pm. QUESTIONS: If you have any questions, please post them here in the event, or contact a.farm.saigon@gmail.com THE SPACE: Established in 2018, A. Farm is a hub for artists, cultural workers, events and exhibitions. Located in a converted factory at the edge of Ho Chi Minh City, long and short-term residencies and public programs conjure ideas and i

title: All Animals Are Equal #1
description: English | Tiếng Việt Để khởi động A. Farm – chương trình lưu trú sáng tác mới tại Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh (TPHCM) do Nguyễn Art Foundation, MoT+++ và Sàn Art điều hành – chúng tôi thân mời tất cả các nghệ sĩ thị giác đang ở TPHCM tham gia triển lãm nhóm “all animals are equal” (tạm dịch: “muôn loài bình đẳng”). Với tinh thần bằng hữu và bình quyền, nghệ sĩ thực hành ở bất kỳ phương tiện nào, tại bất cứ thời điểm nào trong sự nghiệp – dù chuyên hay không chuyên, già hay trẻ, mới xuất hiện hay đã thành danh – đều được mời mang 1-3 tác phẩm và sắp đặt tác phẩm theo ý mình ở bất kỳ vị trí nào trong khu lưu trú A. Farm. Ai đến trước sẽ được chọn không gian trước. Nghệ sĩ có thể đặt tác phẩm tại các vị trí như không gian studio, không gian sinh hoạt của nghệ sĩ và không gian tổ chức triển lãm/sự kiện (vui lòng tham khảo ảnh đăng cùng lời mời này). Các nghệ sĩ video có thể gửi tác phẩm video cho chương trình chiếu phim nhóm. Buổi khai mạc còn có workshop cắt giấy Kirigami, một số tác phẩm trình diễn trong khu studio, đồ uống từ Cù Rú Bar, và kết thúc với một chuỗi trình diễn âm thanh. Đây là dịp cộng đồng nghệ thuật đa dạng ở Sài Gòn đến gần nhau và chia sẻ tác phẩm trên tinh thần đồng đẳng. Danh sách nghệ sĩ sẽ được công bố sau khi nghệ sĩ xác nhận tham gia. Sự kiện này được thực hiện với sự góp phần tài trợ của Institut français & Villa Saigon. ĐỂ ĐĂNG KÝ THAM GIA: Nếu bạn muốn trưng bày tác phẩm trong chương trình, bạn chỉ cần tới và sắp đặt tác phẩm của mình tại A. Farm (địa chỉ: 96/1 Tân Thới Nhất 6, Khu Phố 6, phường Tân Thới Nhất, Quận 12, TPHCM) trong khoảng thời gian từ 10:00h – 20:00h các ngày 17, 18, 19 và 20 tháng Sáu. Chúng tôi có một số dụng cụ cơ bản như búa, khoan và thang, ngoài ra các nghệ sĩ tự chịu trách nhiệm cho vật liệu lắp đặt của mình (ví dụ: dây cáp, đinh vít, giá treo…; bất cứ dụng cụ nào bạn cần, bạn sẽ phải mang để trưng bày tác phẩm của mình). TÁC PHẨM VIDEO: Các nghệ sĩ muốn gửi video để chiếu trong chương trình, vui lòng gửi video tới a.farm.saigon@gmail.com trước ngày 18/6. THÔNG TIN TÁC PHẨM: Các nghệ sĩ tham gia vui lòng gửi email gồm: tên nghệ sĩ, tiêu đề tác phẩm và giá bán (nếu bạn muốn bán tác phẩm) tới a.farm.saigon@gmail.com. Năm mươi phần trăm doanh thu từ việc bán tác phẩm sẽ được dành để trang trải các hoạt động của A. Farm, vì vậy xin vui lòng định giá tác phẩm của bạn một cách phù hợp. THỜI GIAN KHAI MẠC: Ngày 23 tháng Sáu, từ 16:00h – 22:00h; triển lãm sẽ mở cửa cho công chúng từ 12:00h – 17:00h từ ngày 27/6 đến ngày 30/6. THÁO DỠ TÁC PHẨM: Các nghệ sĩ vui lòng đến tháo dỡ tác phẩm vào ngày 2, 3, 4 tháng Bảy, thời gian từ 14:00h đến 20:00h. CÂU HỎI: Nếu bạn có bất kỳ câu hỏi nào, vui lòng đăng trên trang sự kiện, hoặc gửi email tới a.farm.saigon@gmail.com VỀ A. Farm: Thành lập năm 2018, A. Farm là nơi dành cho các nghệ sĩ, người hoạt động văn hoá, sự kiện và triển lãm. Nằm trong không gian của một nhà máy đã được đổi mới bên rìa Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh, các kỳ lưu trú ngắn và dài hạn cùng các sự kiện của A.Farm

title: MoT sound #6
description: english | tiếng việt Our 6th sound experiment will start with an interactive performance titled ‘The Ignorant Schoolmaster‘ conducted by Alex McCarl, in which audience members will be invited to make sounds together using fun and intuitive iPad applications. This will be followed by a live electronic set by French, Shanghai-based artist Loads of Nothing. Finally, we will re-present a collaborative performance titled MAMO by Felipe Calderon Nurmi on the drums, Douglas Schmidt on the sax, and contemporary dancer Nguyen Chung. The evening closes with a group jam session. Tuesday, May 15 6:00 – 8:30 pm 6:00-7:00 pm – The Ignorant Schoolmaster (interactive iPad jam) 7:00-7:30 pm – Loads of Nothing (live electronic set) 7:30-8:00 pm – MAMO (free jazz & contemporary dance) 8:00-8:30 pm – Jam session with all performers location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City. entrance: 100.000 VND MoT sound #6 – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #6
description: english | tiếng việt Thử nghiệm âm thanh thứ 6 của chúng tôi sẽ bắt đầu với một trình diễn tương tác mang tiêu đề “The Ignorant Schoolmaster” (“Ông Giáo Ngốc”) do Alex McCarl thực hiện, trong đó khán giả sẽ được mời tạo âm thanh cùng các ứng dụng iPad trực quan và thú vị. Tiếp theo là set nhạc điện tử của Loads of Nothing, nghệ sĩ người Pháp sống ở Thượng Hải. Cuối cùng, chúng tôi sẽ trình bày lại màn trình diễn mang tính hợp tác có tên MAMO, với Felipe Calderon Nurmi chơi trống, Douglas Schmidt chơi sax, và vũ công đương đại Nguyễn Chung, và một ngẫu hứng nhóm để kết thúc đêm trình diễn. title: Tuyen Nguyen
description: For Tuyen Nguyen, the studio is an intimate subject that provides his artistic experiments with emotion. His paintings are often distorted, deformed and observed from various viewpoints at different times and places to produce new sensations and imaginations. He wishes to express internal depth rather than external form. Tuyen Nguyen – MoT+++ A. title: Alisa Chunchue
description: Alisa has a keen interest in the human body as a testimony to human relationships, livelihood, and social environment. Many of her works rely on medical research, chemistry, science, and calculation. She draws on characteristics of material in order to emphasise the process of physical exploration and the essence of materials. Alisa Chunchue – MoT+++ A. title: MoT sound #5
description: english | tiếng việt The MoT sound art showcase returned for its fifth session. The night began with an improvisation by Nothing+ Trio, consisting of Ben Litwicki, Van Thanh Trung, and Alec Schachner, followed by a live electronic set by a special guest from Hanoi, Nguyen Do Minh Quan, who performed using a modular synthesizer containing elements of his own design. The night closed with a jam session involving all the previous performers, who were also joined by Alex McCarl on the iPads. Location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City. MoT sound #5 – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #4
description: The fourth edition of MoT featured four artists/collectives: Junichi Usui, a Japanese practitioner of traditional Vietnamese music opened the evening with Electronic Đàn Bầu. Hanoi based American sound artist Scobi Wan presented a Feedback Experiment. Thai artist Arnont Nongyao presented a demonstration of DIY Sounds & Visuals, and Saigon based Rắn Cạp Đuôi Collective presented an Improvisational Noise Jam. The evening closed with a jam session including all four artists. Location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City. title: Karen-Thao Nguyen La
description: Karen-Thao Nguyen La is a multi-disciplinary artist and designer from Vancouver, Canada. She currently resides in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, to explore her diasporic lineage. Her practice often finds itself in the liminal space between hyphenated identity. The direction of her physically immersive research is rooted in the commonplace rituals of the public / private sphere, such as work and leisure. She listens, observes and participates—examining the plurality of the body, activities and objects—based on their socioeconomic, geographic and historic context. Karen-Thao Nguyen La – MoT+++ A. title: Lem Trag
description: Lem Trag is a visual artist born in Germany. Growing up between Germany and Vietnam, the intertwined state of Eastern-Western spectacles and cultures have created rich and surreal nuances in her works. Lem does not limit herself to a particular form of art; for her, making art is the space in which she reaches her own discoveries in the course of a creative process that is characterised simultaneously by action and experimentation. Lem Trag – MoT+++ A. title: the Nguyen Art Foundation
displayDate: Established in 2018, the Nguyen Art Foundation was born from a desire to better serve the artistic community of Vietnam. It was founded by Quynh Nguyen, under the advisory of Cam Xanh (pseudonym of Thanh Tran Ha), artist and founder of MoT+++. Since its conception, the foundation has worked to build an alternative infrastructure for […]
description: Established in 2018, the Nguyen Art Foundation was born from a desire to better serve the artistic community of Vietnam. It was founded by Quynh Nguyen, under the advisory of Cam Xanh (pseudonym of Thanh Tran Ha), artist and founder of MoT+++. Since its conception, the foundation has worked to build an alternative infrastructure for the arts in Vietnam, and construct the base for what it hopes will form Vietnam’s first museum of contemporary art. The foundation aims to expand the possibilities for contemporary art in Vietnam by facilitating global exchange that enriches not only individual practices, but engages the overall growth of the Vietnamese art scene. nguyenartfoundation.com the Nguyen Art Foundation – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: Home | Land by Lê Hiền Minh and Cam Xanh | Taiwan Annual Art Fair 2017
description: When technically defined, ‘home’ amounts to little more than the building in which one lives. When defined conceptually however, ‘home’ becomes a feeling that is universally identifiable as one of deep affection and longing. Lê Hiền Minh builds her idea of home from the objects that filled her life when living in the USA: her television, sofa, tables, chairs, pots, bowls, chopsticks, paintbrushes, tubes of paint, handbags, cosmetics… Upon moving back to Vietnam it was the thought of leaving these inanimate objects that evoked more feelings of loss than the building of the house she lived in. For Lê Hiền Minh the objects were a tangible representation of the memories and experiences that made her house a home, and so she chose to cast each of them using traditional Vietnamese Dó paper. Cam Xanh alternatively presents us with the physical home of the silkworm: the silk cocoon. For the silkworm, a cocoon is the sanctuary it builds itself, eager for safety in preparation for its metamorphosis. Whilst we might not build our own houses, its clear from the artists that ‘home’ is an idea of our own construction. What is most striking is how both artists interpret the idea and feeling of home as subject to change. Cam Xanh uses two of Vietnam’s famous Declarations of Independence—the 10 th century poem Nam Quốc Sơn Hà (Mountains and Rivers of the Southern Country) declaring independence from the Chinese, and the 1945 speech from Ho Chi Minh decrying the French and Japanese occupation—to demonstrate the instability of the geopolitical borders that define countries. Whilst both the poem and speech reference the physicality of “our” land for which they are fighting, it is the emphasised emotional connection that makes it worth fighting for. Cam Xanh mounts her work to reflect the arbitrariness of these borders. The cocoons featuring the Vietnamese, French and Japanese translations of Ho Chi Minh’s 1945 speech that resulted in the division of North and South Vietnam stand stoically in two large square boxes—a reminder of the past and its historical influence on the present. Likewise, the smaller, single box containing the poem considered to be Vietnam’s first Declaration of Independence is just one of an unlimited edition. Small though it might be, it suggests an unfinished fight and has significant implications for Vietnam’s, as well as other previously occupied countries, continuing fight for autonomy and dominance in an increasingly globalised, still Western-led world. As a material the silk cocoon is strong despite being very fine, when unravelled it is almost invisible and yet one cocoon contains nearly one kilometre of thread. Like the silkworm, Southeast Asia has a history of being exploited but just as silk exports once played a major role in supporting the region’s economy, Cam Xanh uses the silk cocoon as a symbol to reflect countries such as Vietnam and Taiwan’s ever-expanding reach and presence on the world stage. title: +1 museum by any other name | A. Farm
description: the +1 museum by any other name is a constantly evolving project from MoT+++ that exhibits single artworks in unpredictable locations, for a minimum of one year. evoking the politics of site-specificity and duration, +1 museum artworks use the idiosyncrasies of setting and extended experience of their presence, to create intimate and ongoing conversations with their audiences. by taking the name of the museum, the project questions the role and form of institutions in society, the rise of the exhibition as event, and challenges the standard format of a two-month show period that is now ubiquitous globally. as an alternative, the museum’s artworks are considered carefully and critically in relation to the history and character of each selected place, and encouraged to stay exhibited for as long as possible. as the museum grows worldwide, it creates multiple entry points for exploring the representation and interpretation of art—finding its strength in being simultaneously everywhere and nowhere. it works outside of conventional settings to reject the traditions of the institution and its art, and instead seeks its audiences beyond these accepted boundaries. to mark the beginning of the +1 museum by any other name MoT+++ founder and collective member Cam Xanh placed a single, closed matchbox at an unmarked place in the former Viet Huong factory that became A. Farm in 2018. the factory is one of the biggest exporters from Vietnam, whose manufactured scents and flavours can be found throughout the world. it was converted into the international art residency A. Farm, where its history of scent and flavour exportation is reimagined in the cross-pollination of art and ideas with residents from all over the world living and working together in the space. +1 museum by any other name | A. title: Persona | Nhân dạng | Tuýp Trần
description: In this exhibition, Tuýp Trần raises questions made visible through an exploration of religious, political, and scientific narratives.

title: MoT sound #3
description: Nguyen Hong Giang, a Vietnamese musician, record producer, composer and experimental multi-genre music producer, began the night with an experimental electronic set. Karma Huong, a female artist who has experimented in spirit and meditation, performed with a Tibetian Singing Bowl. The vibration frequency of the bowls had a positive effect on the mental and physical state of all audience members. The night closed with “Spiel”, a live improvisation consisting 4 artists: MadderModes, Karma Huong, KIN, Trung; directed by Nic Ford, a multimedia and electronic music artist responsible for the project ‘MadderModes’. Location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City. MoT sound #3 – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: Renaissance International School now features work by Regis Golay
description: Last month a number of cutting-edge artworks by Regis Golay travelled to Vietnam from Switzerland to be exhibited at Dia Projects, and Renaissance International School instantly purchased one of the video installations displayed at the exhibition to expand the school’s collection of modern art. Golay’s collage of 11 videos is very thought-provoking and has instantly captured the attention of the school’s students, teachers, parents and visitors who enter the “Golay Room”, where the videos are now installed, with a sense of curiosity, attracted by the unusual sounds the videos are producing, and start lively discussions about the videos in the room. The artist, Regis Golay, was born in Geneva in 1978 and currently resides there. He created the Studio Federal in 2005 after graduating with a Bachelor‘s degree in Visual Communication from the ECAL in 2003 with a specialisation in Photography. Accustomed to blurring the boundaries between contemporary art and visual communication, his commissions led him to photography, to designing posters and making videos, as well as to setting up installations for clients as diverse as experimental theater companies, luxury and fashion firms, museums or even cultural events. The context of the videos in the Golay Room at Renaissance only emerges through the videos’ interaction and continuous repetition, and the videos seem to examine and to question continuous, mysterious, and ultimately useless, productivity. The school already has a remarkable collection of modern art, including paintings by star artists like Pham Ha Hai, Pham An Hai, Tran Ngoc Hung and Mai Xuan Oanh, and is building an art gallery next door to its campus in District 7 to ensure its students can benefit from a close-up experience of art as part of their education. Renaissance’s continuing investment in modern art makes it stand out from the crowd, and is highly appreciated by its close-knit community. It is highly unusual to see such installations in the school’s settings, and students tend to joke that they are studying in an art museum rather than in a traditional school. The Board of Directors of the school firmly believe in the power of art to ennoble children and adults and to stimulate their creativity and imagination, and are committed to continue expanding the school’s collection, which has already acquired a great reputation in the city and beyond. Renaissance International School now features work by Regis Golay – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #2
description: Sound artists included Dan Nguyen aka Demon Slayer from LA/Saigon and Nguyen Hong Giang, a Vietnamese musician, record producer, composer and experimental multi-genre music producer. The show also featured ‘Spiel’, a live improvisation by 5 artists: Sonar Lee, MadderModes, Kin, Scobi Wan, Fad Plastic; directed by Nic Ford, a multimedia and electronic music artist responsible for the project ‘ MadderModes ‘. Drinks were served by Cù Rú Bar. Cù Rú Bar is a local artist-run spirits bar inspired by a modern tribe in the north of Lang Biang. The people here live in caves, farm their own food and make their own spirits to share with everybody, anybody. They love to sleep all day. They don’t take bath but love swimming and making love. When feeling inspired they write poems, make paintings and sometimes video art. This is the first time Cù Rú Bar collaborating with MoT+++ in MọT. Location: Saigon Domaine, Ground Floor, 1057 Binh Quoi, Ward 28, Binh Thanh Dist., Ho Chi Minh City MoT sound #2 – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: MoT sound #1
description: MoT # 1 is the first event from Vietnam’s first ever project dedicated solely to experimental sound performance. The project seeks to expand interpretations of sound art through mediums such as recordings, performance, and sculpture. The show started with a sound installation by Nic Ford, a multimedia and electronic music artist responsible for the project ‘MadderModes‘ and Andrew Piggott, a filmmaker. Followed by a performance by Cam Xanh and then a solo by sound artist Scobi Wan. A sound conversation, directed by Nic Ford, together with other artists and producers: Sonar Lee, MadderModes, Kin, Scobi Wan, Fad Plastic introduced how sounds find and connect with each other through improvisational performance. Location: Belgo – 159A Nguyễn Văn Thủ Street, Dakao Ward, Dist. 1, Ho Chi Minh City MoT sound #1 – MoT+++ programs collective MoTsound A. title: Islands, Archipelagos and Other Liquid Territories
description: MAP Office (Laurent Gutierrez and Valérie Portefaix) presented 20 years of reflection on territories, unfolding their navigations and long-time reading of unstable global geographies.

title: The Primacy of Drawing
location: 103 Dong Khoi, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City
description:
First comes drawing. Drawings in caves dating back to prehistory mark our earliest experiences as humans and within the span of our own lives, it is often the ability to communicate through the drawn image that develops before articulate speech. And as adults, it is the drawings that first record Galileo's observations of Saturn and within the spaces of imagination and story, drawings have the ability to visualize the most fantastical concepts. The adage that "a picture is worth a thousand words" speaks to the power of the image as a primary language.

Drawing is often the first mark and impulse after the thought. It is also marks the beginning of a process whereby the artist is able to hone and refine their concepts. Like the house that Matisse speaks of, it is the foundation upon which the work is built.

In this exhibition, we look to five artists who take very different approaches to drawing both in intent, material, and content. Their relation to the creative process will be traced through finished drawings that co-exist with sketches, prints, and installations. The work begins with the drawing, both for the featured artists and the inaugural opening of our space.

Artists: Lys Bui, Hoang Nam Viet, Sandrine Llouquet, Ayano Otani, Mai Thi Tran
